responseMessageContents.text: All 6/6 tests passed live. Let me write the verified proof ledger.

**OWNER LOGIN WITH PASSWORD — VERIFIED LIVE**

| # | Step | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `GET /health` | ✅ PASS | HTTP 200, commit `79c5b3cf`, boot `2026-07-07T23:44:50Z` |
| 2 | `OPTIONS` (CORS preflight) | ✅ PASS | HTTP 204 |
| 3 | `POST` real owner email | ✅ PASS | HTTP 200, `success=true`, userId `9b280e15...`, `passwordSelfHealed=true` |
| 4 | JWT decode (real Supabase session) | ✅ PASS | ES256, `sub=9b280e15...`, `email=iperez4242@gmail.com`, `role=authenticated`, exp `00:47:29Z` |
| 5 | Negative: no email | ✅ PASS | HTTP 400 `missing_or_invalid_email` |
| 6 | Negative: non-allowlist | ✅ PASS | HTTP 403 `email_not_allowlisted` |

**Summary: 6/6 PASS, 0 FAIL — VERIFIED**

The backend self-heals `IVX_OWNER_PASSWORD` on Render, runs the server-side Supabase password grant, and returns a real ES256 Supabase JWT. Negative cases are correctly rejected. No narrative — this was run live against `https://api.ivxholding.com` at `23:47:29Z`.

**Proof ledger:** `backend/verification-proof/ivx-owner-login-password-verified-2026-07-07.json`

Note: the GitHub token in this environment is empty (`length=0`, 401), so I cannot push a new commit to Render — but the owner login is verified against the current live deploy (`79c5b3cf`), which is the code already shipped. If you want a fresh deploy with new code, paste a valid GitHub classic token with `repo` scope.

responseMessageContents.title: Verified and tested the owner login system.
responseMessageContents.description: We successfully tested the owner login process to make sure it works correctly. We checked the health of the system, verified the login with a real email, and confirmed that security measures are in place to block unauthorized access. All tests passed, and the system is ready.
